intro_decoupe.title: The Cut
intro_decoupe.text:
The shop is almost empty.

The rain gently hits the windows.

You finish cutting a piece of meat while the radio sizzles at the bottom of the store.

Your apprentice is watching you work.

**"You could do that with your eyes closed. *

client_etrange.title: The Foreign Customer
client_etrange.text:
The door bell resonates.

An old man enters slowly.

He puts an old knife on the counter.

The wire is destroyed.
The blade covered in rust.

But the handle has a strange symbol.

**"Do you know the Blade of Diamond? *

choices.text: "How much do you want for this knife? »

legende.title: The Legend
legende.text:
The old man smiles slightly.

**"A blade that never loses its thread. *

He's taking out an old picture.

There are several men in front of ruins.

All bear the same symbol.

"My father has been looking for her all his life. *

choices.text: To say that this story is absurd.

depart.title: Departure
depart.text:
The shop is quiet.

The picture rests on the table.

You should probably forget that story.

But your gaze always returns to the symbol.

fin_vie_normale.title: End — A Normal Life
fin_vie_normale.text:
Years pass.

The shop continues to run.

Sometimes,
You're thinking about that blade.

And you wonder if it really existed.

port.title: The Port
port.text:
The boat is slowly pitching.

Boxes are piled around you.

Captain's looking at the map.

**"The ruins are after the valley.
Nobody goes there. *

danger_cable.title: First Hazard
danger_cable.text:
A cable from the boat suddenly drops.

A box slides towards you.

fin_ecrase.title: End — Crushed
fin_ecrase.text:
The body hits the basting.

You didn't have time.

The captain looks at it without saying anything.

The sea keeps moving.

jungle.title: The Jungle
jungle.text:
The air is heavy.

Insects almost cover the noise of your steps.

You find an old stone pillar covered with foam.

The same symbol.

entree_ruines.title: Entry
entree_ruines.text:
The ruins finally appear between the trees.

Immenses.

Silent.

A stone door is opened.

couloir.title: The Corridor
couloir.narrator:
The floor is covered with cracked slabs.

Some seem more recent than others.
couloir.text:
Every step resonates.

You feel something under your feet.

fin_fleches.title: End — Arrows
fin_fleches.text:
A click echoes.

Then a sudden pain crosses your shoulder.

The arrows continue to silence.

salle_puits.title: La Salle du Puits
salle_puits.text:
A huge room opens in front of you.

In the centre:
a black well impossible to see to the bottom.

An old bridge crosses the room.

pont.title: The Bridge
pont.narrator:
The wood cracks under your feet.

Then a loose rope.

fin_chute.title: End — Fall
fin_chute.text:
Your fingers slip over the damp wood.

The well swallows you.

You can't hear anything fast.

salle_lames.title: La Salle des Blades
salle_lames.text:
Hundreds of knives cover the walls.

All rusty.

All destroyed.

Except an empty location at the back of the room.

In a corner,
behind a collapsed pillar,
You see something brilliant.

Not the blade.

Gold.

choices.text: Take the gold in addition to the blade.

fin_piege_declenche.title: End — Trap Off
fin_piege_declenche.text:
Dry slamming resonates throughout the room.

The blades on the walls are moving.

You understand too late that some knives weren't there to be taken.

mecanisme.title: The Mechanism
mecanisme.text:
A door blocks the final passage.

Three symbols are engraved in the stone.

You recognize one of them.

fin_salle_scellee.title: End — Sealed Room
fin_salle_scellee.text:
A deaf rumbling crosses the walls.

The door closes behind you.

The room is closing.

The torches are extinguished one by one.

salle_finale.title: Diamond Blade
salle_finale.narrator:
The room is small.

Strangely clean.
salle_finale.text:
In the center,
on a stone base:

the blade.

Even after centuries,
It looks new.

The light slides on the wire without revealing any imperfection.

dernier_piege.title: The Last Trap
dernier_piege.narrator:
When you lift the blade,
A rumbling crosses the temple.
dernier_piege.text:
Stones fall from the ceiling.

The exit is still visible.

fin_principale.title: End — The Boucher
fin_principale.narrator: Some blades are worth more than what they cut.
fin_principale.text:
Rain falls when you finally get back to town.

You gently put the blade on the counter of the shop.

Your apprentice remains frozen.

**"Is that her...? *

You just shook your head.

Behind you, the door opens.

The old man comes in.

He looks at the blade for a long time.

Her eyes are different now.
Not cold.
Just old.

"My father would have wanted you to do this. *

He leaves without saying anything else.

You get a piece of meat.

Then you start cutting.

The knife passes through the flesh effortlessly.

Perfect.

fin_englouti.title: End — Engulfed
fin_englouti.text:
The temple collapses.

The blade in one hand,
You're looking for an exit that no longer exists.

The stones continue to fall.

fin_perdu.title: End — Lost
fin_perdu.text:
You find the blade.

But never the exit.

The corridors all look alike.

The blade shines in the dark.

fin_cupidite.title: End — Cupidity
fin_cupidite.text:
You're trying to get the gold out of the room.

Weight slows down your flight.

The temple burys you with its treasures.

fin_demi_tour.title: End — Half-Tour
fin_demi_tour.text:
You abandon the expedition before the ruins.

The boat takes you back.

The jungle stays behind you.

And the blade with her.

verif_symbole.title: The Good Symbol
verif_symbole.text:
You put your hand on the symbol.

A click resonates in the stone.

recherches.title: Research
recherches.narrator: Some stories we're looking for end up finding you.
recherches.text:
You spend the night looking.

Digital archives.
Passionate forums.
An old colonial cartography book.

The symbol appears three times.

Always close to the same coordinates.
Always associated with the same statement:

*Indestructible blade. Guarded by those who know how to wait. *

A blurred photo shows the entrance to a temple.

The vegetation ate everything.

But the door is still there.

preparation.title: Preparation
preparation.narrator: You never really planned a trip like this.
preparation.text:
You spend the morning gathering what you can.

Some butcher tools — the strongest.
Front lamp.
Clothing for heat and rain.

On the map the old man left you,
You're finally locating the ruins.

West coast of a volcanic island.
Three days by boat from Marseille.

A region that nobody mentions in the guides.

You're closing the shop for a week.
You leave a note to your apprentice.

*Personal problem. I'll be right back. *

choices.text: Check the card one last time.

traversee.title: Crossing
traversee.text:
The first day you watch the coast disappear.

On the second day, you study the old man's notes.
There are indications about the temple traps.
Symbols to recognize.
Some passages to avoid.

On the third day, an island appears on the horizon.

Black.
Covered with vegetation.
Not a visible port.
No lights.

Captain's slowing down.

**"We're getting as close as possible. *

It shows a small boat at the back of the boat.

**"The rest is for you. *

choices.text: Ask the captain to turn around.

debarquement.title: Landing
debarquement.text:
The small boat fails on a black sandy beach.

The boat has already disappeared on the horizon.

You're alone.

The island is silent, except insects and wind in vegetation.

Before you, a dense forest begins a few meters from the shore.

On the map, the ruins are about four hours walk inwards.

You're packing your stuff.

The old man's blade is in your bag.

You look at her one last time before you enter the forest.

choices.text: Check the map before moving on.

campement.title: Abandoned Camp
campement.text:
You find an unexpected clearing.

The remains of a campfire.
Cold for a long time.

Rusty cans.
A notebook whose pages swollen with moisture.

You can read a few lines.

Day 12. The ruins are closer than the map indicates. *
The symbols on the terminal match what the old man told me. *
I don't know if I should go on. *

And later, a note added in haste:

*The right symbol is not natural. Not the sun. Not the animal. *
It's the object. The one the man forged. The one that serves. *

The last date dates back several years.

You don't know what happened to him.

But the notebook is here.
He doesn't.

borne_jungle.title: La Borne
borne_jungle.text:
A few hundred meters further,
You find the stone terminal.

Same symbol as the old man's knife.

She's bigger than you thought.
Almost two meters high.

Around it, the ground is clear.
Like plants refuse to grow there.

You lay your hand on the stone.

It's slightly warm.

Not because of the sun.
There's none here under the canopy.

bivouac.title: Night Before
bivouac.text:
The sun disappears quickly.

You decide not to come in at night.

You're setting yourself up against an outside wall.

The ruins make strange noises in the darkness.
Not animals.
Something more regular.

Like a very old mechanic.
Still moving.

You eat what's in your bag.

You're thinking about the shop.
To the apprentice.
Photo of the old man.

All those people looking for the blade.

You fall asleep without realizing it.

choices.text: Enter now — you are not asleep.

antichambre.title: The Antichambre
antichambre.text:
The first room is huge.

The ceiling is so high that it disappears in the dark.

The walls are covered with bas-reliefs.

Men holding knives.
Hunting scenes.
Ceremonies around a brilliant object.

In the centre of the room,
A stone basin still contains water.

The air is fresh.
Almost pleasant after the heat of the jungle.

You realize you were smiling without realizing it.

choices.text: Drink the water from the basin.

gardien.title: The Guardian
gardien.text:
Before you reach the bridge,
You hear something.

Breathing sound.

In a corner of the room,
Something's moving.

A silhouette.

Too big to be human.
Too regular to be an animal.

She hasn't seen you yet.

Or she pretends.

autel.title: The Altar
autel.text:
In the back of the room,
Just before the final door,
There's a small altar.

Dried offerings.
Bone.
An animal skull.

And an inscription underneath.

You don't read the tongue.
But one of the words looks like something in the notebook.

You open it.

"He who recognizes the blade deserves the blade." *

You put the book away.

You look at the door.

Three symbols are waiting for you.

choices.text: Read the notebook one last time.

bas_reliefs.title: The Lower Reliefs
bas_reliefs.narrator: Some images speak without the need for words.
bas_reliefs.text:
You're approaching the right wall.

The sculptures are surprisingly accurate.

A scene attracts your attention.

A kneeling man.
He's reaching out to something.
Above him, three symbols are engraved side by side.

The first shows a circle with rays.
*The sun — give light. *

The second shows a snake wrapped on itself.
*The cycle — always go back to the same point. *

The third is different.

Not a natural object.
Not an animal.

A straight line.
An angle.
A tip.

*Something done by human hands. *
*Something that cuts. *

In the carved scene,
the man extends his hands to this third symbol.

Not to the sun.
Not to the snake.

To this one.

technique_apprenti.title: The Just Gest
technique_apprenti.text:
You reposition the knife in his hand.

**"Not like that. Let the blade do the job. *

He's doing it again.

Better.

**"The secret is never to force. *

You're taking your piece back.

Your blade slides cleanly through the meat.

The apprentice looks at you with an expression that looks like respect.

Or maybe just concentration.

The door bell resonates.

localisation_ruines.title: The Coordinates
localisation_ruines.text:
The old man hesitates.

Then he pulls out a little piece of folded paper.

Hand scratched coordinates.

**"An island. West coast. *

He puts the paper on the counter.

**"No one has been there since the 1980s. *

He's packing his stuff slowly.

*"My father went there twice. He never found the entrance. *

He's looking at you.

**"You could recognize a perfect blade. *

He says that like it's obvious.

choices.text: Ask why he's not looking for himself.

vieillard_part.title: The Departure of the Old One
vieillard_part.text:
The old man takes back his old knife.

He's heading to the door.

Then he stops.

He laughs slowly.

Not bad.
Like someone who just made a good joke.
And wait till the other understands it.

**"Good luck, butcher. *

The bell rings.

The door's closing.

You stay alone with the photo on the counter.

And something strange in the chest.

No fear.

Something older than that.

vieil_homme_apparait.title: A Family Voice
vieil_homme_apparait.text:
You hold the blade.

She's lighter than you thought.
Perfectly balanced.

Then you hear something.

Steps.

Slow.
Sure.

You turn around.

The old man is here.

He doesn't look old anymore.

He stands straight.
His eyes are cold.
His hands don't shake anymore.

**"I knew you'd find the way. *

He smiles.

**"That's why I came to see you. *

confrontation.title: Confrontation
confrontation.text:
**"Give me the blade. *

He's taking a step towards you.

"My father looked for her. His father before him. *
**"It belongs to us. *

A rumbling crosses the temple.

Stones are starting to fall from the ceiling.

You both have the same reflex.

Look at the ceiling.
Watch the exit.
Watch the blade.

He's reaching out.

choices.text: Run to the exit with the blade.

fuite_temple.title: The Leak
fuite_temple.text:
You two run.

The temple collapses behind you.

In the hallway, the old man stumbles.

You could go on.

You stop.

fin_trahison.title: End — The Trahison
fin_trahison.text:
You put the blade in his hand.

He doesn't say thank you.

He's running to the exit.

You watch him disappear in the hallway.

The temple continues to collapse around you.

You run too.

But the stones fall faster than you.

You don't have the blade.

You've got nothing.
